<!-- GENERATED by skills/generate.js from openapi.yaml (cross_lens_verify). DO NOT EDIT BY HAND. -->

# Verify an actor

You need to understand or attest a destination's identity and trustworthiness.
This is the **investigation / attestation** call — it answers "who is this,
really, and do its claims hold?" For a go/no-go gate on an imminent action,
use `preflight-should-i-act` instead.

## When to run this

- **Due diligence** on a counterparty or a supply-chain partner before onboarding.
- **Attribution** — naming who is behind an IP, domain, ASN, or entity.
- **Durable attestation** — recording a verdict you'll need to defend later
  (audit, compliance, incident write-up).
- Any time you need the **identity and the evidence**, not just allow/deny.

## How to call

Prefer the MCP tool if `mcp-data.tunnelmind.ai` is connected:

```
cross_lens_verify({
  node: "nytimes.com",
  weights: { ... },   // optional
  thresholds: { ... },   // optional
  ait: "AIT-0192f5d3-2c1e-7af6-bd84-9c4a3e8b7d12",   // optional
})
```

Otherwise call the HTTP endpoint directly (the node is in the path):

```
POST https://data.tunnelmind.ai/v1/verify/nytimes.com
Content-Type: application/json

{}
```

### Request fields

| field | required | meaning |
|-------|----------|---------|
| `node` | yes | The node to verify. Type is auto-detected: IPv4/IPv6 address, domain, ASN with optional `AS` prefix, or entity_slug. |
| `weights` | no | Per-component weight overrides |
| `thresholds` | no | { pass, fail } verdict cutoffs (defaults 0.7 / 0.3) |
| `ait` | no | Optional ATAP AIT id (`AIT-<uuidv7>`). When present, the verdict is wrapped in a witness-tier `cross_lens:verified` event chained onto the AIT and signed by Sigil (witness OAI-2026-0000201). Independent of the AIT, a short-lived signed `sigil_token` is always issued on a successful verify, and a durable TunnelMind `receipt` (v1.0) commits to the verdict for long-term audit. |

## How to read the result

The fused verdict is in `cross_lens`:

| `cross_lens.verdict` | What it means |
|----------------------|---------------|
| `pass` | Claims hold across the lenses that had data. Safe to rely on, at the stated confidence. |
| `warn` | Mixed or thin signal. Treat as unverified — surface the `signals` and seek corroboration before relying on it. |
| `fail` | A lens actively contradicts the claim, or a hard floor tripped. Do not rely on this actor; report why. |
| `unknown` | No lens produced a usable signal. State that the actor could not be verified — absence of data is not a clean bill. |

- **Weight your conclusion by `cross_lens.confidence`.** It reflects how many
  lenses had data (~0.55 one lens / ~0.80 two / ~0.94 three). Low confidence
  means thin coverage, not a clean result — say so explicitly.
- **`cross_lens.adversary_class.classification` names WHO** — one of
  `surveillance_bigtech`, `human_hacker`, `rogue_agent`, `clean`, `unknown`. It is asserted only from a curated
  record or a live observation, **never inferred from absence**. When it's set,
  report it together with its `anchored_by` and `evidence`.

Hard floors override the headline score — if `signals`/`reasons` mention `ghostroute.rpki=INVALID` (possible BGP hijack) or `sanctions_match`, treat it as the worst verdict regardless of the number.

## Keep the durable receipt

Unlike pre-flight's 5-minute consultation token, verify returns a permanent
TunnelMind **`receipt`** (v1.0) that commits to node + verdict + trust_score,
Ed25519-signed. **Attach it to your audit log as independently re-checkable
evidence of what was decided and witnessed** — it can be verified offline at
`https://tunnelmind.ai/standards/receipt-format/v1`. If you passed an `ait`,
a witness-tier `cross_lens:verified` event is also chained onto the AIT;
reference its `id`.
